commit.message:
Use gh auth setup-git for authenticated git push in CD workflow (#223)

* fix(cd): use app token explicitly for release push

The stefanzweifel/git-auto-commit-action may override the credential
setup from the github-app-auth action, causing the final git push to
use the default GITHUB_TOKEN instead of the app token. This means the
push lacks branch protection bypass permissions.

Fix by explicitly setting the remote URL with the app token before
pushing, ensuring the automation app's credentials are used.

https://claude.ai/code/session_0189gHoRYFdoYF2gtzBB5JLM

* fix(cd): use gh auth setup-git instead of embedding token in URL

Cleaner approach: re-establish the gh credential helper right before
push with the app token explicitly in the environment. This avoids
embedding tokens in the remote URL.

https://claude.ai/code/session_0189gHoRYFdoYF2gtzBB5JLM

Fix: don't abort in-flight claude process on new keystrokes

Previously, any keystroke while claude was running would abort the
process and restart the 1.5s debounce timer, meaning suggestions
could never arrive if the user kept typing. Now the claude process
runs to completion while new requests are queued. After completion,
if new requests arrived, the debounce timer restarts.

commit.message:
fix: replace rasterization with DOM-based SVG text for question export

Replace the rejected foreignObject rasterization approach with an improved
SVG text approach that uses browser-accurate DOM-based word wrapping and
preserves inline formatting (bold/italic) via SVG <tspan> elements.

Key changes:
- wrapHtmlToFormattedLines(): renders HTML in a hidden div at exact width,
  walks text nodes char-by-char using Range.getClientRects() to detect
  browser line breaks (matches CSS layout exactly, unlike canvas.measureText)
- parseHtmlParagraphs(): splits TipTap HTML into per-paragraph content
- createFormattedSvgText(): creates SVG <text> with <tspan> children for
  bold (<strong>) and italic (<em>) formatting
- Removed: sanitizeForXhtml, buildQuestionXHTML, rasterizeQuestionBlock
  (the rejected rasterization approach)
- measureQuestionBlock is now synchronous (no more async/await)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Root cause: .so files (pydantic_core, uvloop, httptools) compiled for
cpython-312 on GitHub runner, but Azure runs Python 3.13.

PublicationCard, Patent, PatentCard, BlogPost, and Consultancy components.

* fix: add YAML string escaping to prevent malformed PROJECT.md

Add proper YAML string escaping to ProjectMarkdownParser.generate() to
prevent LLM-generated text with special characters from breaking YAML
parsing.

Changes:
- ProjectMarkdownParser: Add escapeYAMLString() helper method
  - Detects strings needing quoting (quotes, colons, apostrophes, etc.)
  - Escapes backslashes, double quotes, newlines, tabs
  - Only quotes when necessary (valid unquoted strings remain unquoted)
- Apply escaping to all string fields: title, author, description, genre,
  tags, episodesDir, audioDir, exportFormat, cast members, hooks, TTS config
- Update test expectations for hook fields (no longer always quoted)

Problem:
LLM-generated descriptions often contain unbalanced quotes, colons, and
apostrophes (e.g., "Podcast: Meditations" or "project'shared"), which
breaks YAML parsing.

Solution:
Automatic YAML string escaping that:
- Detects special characters: \"':{}[],&*#?|<>=!%@\`
- Quotes strings with leading/trailing whitespace
- Escapes quotes, backslashes, newlines, tabs
- Preserves simple strings without unnecessary quoting

Tested with problematic LLM output:
- ✓ "Podcast: Meditations" → quoted and valid
- ✓ "project'shared" → quoted and valid
- ✓ Unbalanced quotes properly escaped
- ✓ All 38 ProjectMarkdownParser tests pass

fix(matrixci): fix cols_times_pivot_inv backward substitution (issue #259) (#260)

* fix(matrixci): fix backward substitution in cols_times_pivot_inv

The method solves X * L_pivot = L_sub where L_pivot is lower triangular.
The previous code used forward substitution (k in 0..j), but since L_pivot
is lower triangular, those entries are zero -- making the substitution a
no-op. Changed to backward substitution (j from n-1 to 0, k in j+1..n)
to correctly access the non-zero lower-triangular entries.

This caused incorrect reconstruction in MatrixLUCI::left() when
left_orthogonal=true, which propagated to TCI2 full-grid evaluation
errors (issue #259).

Closes #259
